content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 30, 1990


Wendy Leece
1107 Valley Circle
Costa Mesa, CA 92627

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-90-061

Dear Ms. Leece:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding your
responsibilities as a candidate for elective office under the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your request seeks
general guidance regarding the permissible uses of campaign funds,
we are treating your request as one for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).²

QUESTION

You ran unsuccessfully for school board in November 1989. In
anticipation of a future candidacy, can you use the funds
remaining in the campaign bank account for the following
expenditures:

1. Subscriptions to periodicals regarding education policy?

2. Membership in organizations to benefit your future
candidacy?

3. Your future candidacies for office other than for the
school board?

___________________________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-90-061
Page 2

CONCLUSION

Surplus campaign funds which were raised after January 1,
1989, may be used as permitted by Section 85807. Expenditure of
such funds to benefit your future candidacy is not permitted by
Section 85807.

FACTS

You ran unsuccessfully for Newport-Mesa Unified School
District Board of Trustees election in November, 1989. Your
campaign bank account now has a balance of approximately $1,000.
These funds were raised after January 1, 1989.

ANALYSIS

Section 85807 provides:

Upon leaving any elected office, or at the end of
the postelection reporting period following the
defeat of a candidate for elective office,
whichever occurs last, campaign funds raised after
January 1, 1989, under the control of the former
candidate or elected officer shall be considered
surplus campaign funds and shall be disclosed
pursuant to the requirements of Chapter 4
(commencing with Section 84100) and shall be [used]
only for the following purposes:

(a) The payment of outstanding campaign debts
or elected officer's expenses.

(b) The pro rata repayment of contributions.

(c) Donations to any bona fide charitable,
educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-
exempt, nonprofit organization, where no
substantial part of the proceeds will have a
material financial effect on the former candidate
or elected officer, any member of his or her
immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer.

(d) Contributions to a political party or
committee so long as the funds are not used to make
contributions in support of or opposition to a
candidate for elective office.

(e) Contributions to support or oppose any
candidate for federal office, any candidate for
elective office in a state other than California,
or any ballot measure.

Our File No. I-90-061
Page 3

During your telephone conversation of February 12, 1990 with
Ms. Lilly Spitz of our office, you stated that all the funds now
remaining in your campaign bank account were raised after January
1, 1989. Consequently, Section 85807 would dictate that you may
not use the funds remaining in your campaign account to further
your future candidacy, unless the use falls within the specific
provisions of Section 85807.

The expenditures for periodical subscriptions and
organizational memberships outlined in your question would be in
anticipation of running for the office of school board, once
again. Such expenditures are not specifically permitted by
Section 85807 and are, therefore, prohibited.³ Assuming that you
have no plans to run for federal office, you should follow the
provisions of Section 85807 to disburse the surplus funds
remaining in your campaign account.

If you have any questions regarding this analysis, please
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature: Jeevan Ahuja]

By: Jeevan Ahuja
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSA:aa

Enclosure

_______________________
3 The Commission has recently assumed jurisdiction over the laws
regarding the permissible uses of campaign funds. In the near
future, the Commission will begin considering and approving
regulations interpreting these laws. Therefore, it is possible
that the Commission may adopt regulations in the future which may
be a variance with the advice given in this letter.
